claim: HN engineers treat the absence of an RSS feed as a strong negative signal — corporate blogs without RSS are presumed to be marketing-led with no engineer input.

claim: Current's developer Terry Godier argues that inbox-style feed readers manufacture obligation anxiety because RSS items aren't messages waiting for a reply.
quote: Email's unread count means something specific: these are messages from real people who wrote to you and are, in some cases, actively waiting for your response. But when we applied that same visual language to RSS…we imported the anxiety without the cause.

claim: RSS reader adoption grew 34% YoY in 2026 while Substack hit 8.4M paid subs (up 68%), and the average person now juggles 20–30 newsletters against 121 daily emails — driving demand for triage tools.
quote: Substack alone crossing 8.4 million paid subscribers in Q1 2026 — a 68% year-over-year jump — and RSS adoption climbing 34% YoY

claim: A widely-circulated 2026 unsubscribe rubric drops any newsletter unopened for 30+ days, duplicating social media, generic, or unrelated to current goals — only ~24% of received emails are judged actually important.
quote: Only 24% of emails are actually important—76% is noise. 62% of daily emails are newsletters, notifications, or irrelevant forwards

claim: The dominant 2026 advice is a hard cap of 2–3 AI newsletters: one daily briefing plus 1–2 weekly deep dives, matched to role (TLDR AI for engineers, Rundown for executives, The Neuron for non-technical).
quote: Subscribe to 2-3 AI newsletters maximum: one daily briefing (The Rundown AI or Superhuman AI) plus 1-2 weekly deep dives matched to your specific interests.

quote: AI slop refers to mass-produced, low-value content created using artificial intelligence tools—content that exists just to fill space and grab attention rather than to inform or help.

claim: Hacker News updated its guidelines in 2026 to ban AI-generated or AI-edited comments outright, codifying the community heuristic that human-written prose — even with broken English — beats polished slop.
quote: Don't post generated comments or AI-edited comments. HN is for conversation between humans.

quote: researchers at the cybersecurity firm DoubleVerify identified a network of more than 200 websites publishing AI-generated content. The operation, called "AutoBait", used templated prompts in a large language model

claim: Marc Brooker's much-shared March 2026 post argues many engineering heuristics are now wrong post-LLM, and the reader's job is humility plus active re-evaluation — applies directly to which blogs still earn a slot.
quote: Engineers need to have humility to accept that many of their heuristics are wrong, courage to believe some are still right, and curiosity to actively learn the difference.
